claim: All-India demand met tops 270 GW at least once this week.
basis: Our own sampling already caught 269.4 GW on 17 Jul; a monsoon lull with high heat-and-humidity load usually nudges the national peak past 270 GW.

claim: Renewables' share of generation tops 34% on at least one midday this week.
basis: We observed 32.8% on 17 Jul; clear post-monsoon afternoons lift solar output and push the instantaneous renewable share higher.

claim: All-India demand met tops 269 GW at least once in the next 7 days.
basis: Auto-generated from our sampling: daily peaks have reached about 269 GW over the last two weeks, so a similar high in the week ahead is the call.

claim: India's grid peak demand will exceed 275 GW at least once in the next 7 days.
basis: AI call (GLM): a recent peak of 269 GW combined with persistent monsoon humidity keeps pushing cooling and industrial load up, so a fresh high above 275 GW is likely.
